matches.moment: Kane exploits Panama's high defensive line with a first-time turn after Bellingham's through ball; Panama never recover from conceding before the half-hour mark
Croatia score from a Perišić cross-set piece; Ghana respond when Partey drives forward, plays Semenyo in behind Stanišić, and the Bristol City winger finishes low to Livaković's right

matches.venue: Hard Rock Stadium, Miami Gardens, FL
matches.moment: Ronaldo's timed run from deep — overlooked by Colombia's compact block — meets Bruno Fernandes's diagonal; the header is unstoppable and sets the pattern for Portugal's patient group-stage win
Wissa's relentless first-half press forces two Uzbekistan errors in the opening 20 minutes; DR Congo score twice from direct pressing turnovers before Uzbekistan can organize

matches.venue: GEHA Field at Arrowhead Stadium, Kansas City, MO
matches.moment: Mahrez's precisely placed free kick from 25 yards deceives the Austrian wall and finds the top-right corner; Austria, unwilling to overcommit with their GD position secure, concede a Sabitzer counter-attack equalizer

Álvarez, in his first start after an ankle absence, channels full pressing intensity in the high block in the first half — his close control in a tight 30-yard channel leads directly to Argentina's opener; Messi enters at 60' to sustained applause

verdict: Today's six-match final matchday closes three of the twelve groups, and the data tells a clear story: the quality gaps between today's combatants are wider than the group-stage records suggest. England dispatched Croatia in their opener but were stopped by Ghana's defensive fortress — today they face a Panama side that has produced literally zero goals and zero quality chances across two matches; the 3-0 outcome is the most arithmetic prediction on the slate. The match that matters for Group L is Croatia vs Ghana — a collision between Croatia's creative desperation and Ghana's two-game zero-conceded record that the 1-1 draw outcome reflects with structural logic: Croatia score from a set piece, Ghana answer on the counter with Semenyo's pace, and the Black Stars advance while Croatia scrambles for a third-place route. In Group K, Colombia's tournament-leading six points mask a significant attacking quality flaw — their 0.052 xG-per-shot against DR Congo is the tournament's most important under-the-surface number, and Portugal's Ronaldo-powered attack exploits it for a 2-1 victory that hands Ronaldo his seventh World Cup tournament goal. DR Congo's assault on Uzbekistan is the most structurally lopsided match of the day — Wissa's 162 pressures meeting a team with 11 opposition-box touches — and three goals and a best-third-place candidacy for the Leopards is the predictable result. Group J provides the day's most charged atmosphere: Algeria vs Austria carries the weight of the 1982 Disgrace of Gijón and the raw necessity of both sides needing something — the 1-1 draw is the most probable single outcome and the one that, brutally, leaves Algeria relying on the best-third-place lottery while Austria advance on goal difference. Meanwhile in Arlington, a rotated Argentina dispatches Jordan efficiently without Messi for 60 minutes, giving Julián Álvarez his first start and Lautaro Martínez another goal in what is ultimately a training exercise for the defending champions. — The Analyst.

players.name: Harry Kane
players.stat: Scored 4 goals vs Panama at Russia 2018; England 6.72 xG in 2 tournament games
players.watch: First-touch finish inside the box; aerial threat from set pieces vs Panama's passive CBs

players.name: Cristiano Ronaldo
players.stat: 2.1 non-penalty xG after 2 matches — highest outside Haaland & Jonathan David at tournament; 2 goals vs Uzbekistan
players.watch: Diagonal run behind Colombia's left-back; header from crossing position; late box arrival

players.name: Yoane Wissa
players.stat: 162 pressures applied (tournament leader); 49 in final third; 5 opposition-box touches
players.watch: Winning the ball in Uzbekistan's defensive third via pressing turnovers; second-ball finishing

players.name: Riyad Mahrez
players.stat: Captain; Algeria's sole creative nucleus; provided assist vs Jordan; free kick specialist
players.watch: Direct cut from right to left-foot shot angle; free kick placement over Austrian wall

players.name: Antoine Semenyo
players.stat: 11 EPL goals in 2024/25 (Bristol City → Bournemouth); Ghana's fastest attacking outlet
players.watch: Running in behind Croatia's high press-then-recover line; left-side cut-across finish

players.name: Julián Álvarez
players.stat: First start of tournament after ankle injury; fallback: 0.56 xG/90 and 22 goals at Atlético Madrid 2024/25
players.watch: Pressing in Jordan's half leading directly to a chance; combination play with Lautaro in tight space
